--- Commit Message ---
LU-11023 quota: quota pools for OSTs

Patch allows to apply quota settings
not only for the whole system, but also
for different OST pools. Since this patch
each "LOD" pool is duplicated by QMT.
Thus quota pools(QP) could be tuned by
standard lctl pool_new/add/remove/erase
commands. All QPs are subset of a global pool
that includes all data devices in a system,
including DOM. However DOM is not supported.
I don't see a lot of work to add DOM support
in future - just need to decide how MDTs
could be combined in a pool.

The main idea of QP is to find all pools
for requested ID(usr/grp/prj) and apply
minimum limit. The patch doesn't affect
qsd side, so slaves know nothing about
pools and different limits. Qunit and edquot
are calculated for each slave on master.

To apply quota on QP, the patch adds key "-o"
to lfs setquota. To get quotas for QP, it
provides long option "--pool" in lfs quota.
See examples of using in sanity-quota_1b/c/d.

Now QPs work properly only on a clean system.
Support of recalculation granted space in case
of adding/removing OSTs in a pool will be added
in the next patch together with accounting
already granted space by each ID in a POOl.

Test-Parameters: testgroup=review-dne-part-4

--- Message (Sergey Cheremencev, 2020-01-28 18:17:14.000000000) ---

--- Message (Andreas Dilger, 2020-01-29 02:29:18.000000000) ---
Patch Set 31:

(1 comment)

Mike, could you please comment on the best way to extend the protocol here for maximum compatibility.  My understanding is that we should be able to *increase* the size of any buffer, or add a new buffer to a message without problems, assuming that the peer can safely ignore that buffer if it doesn't know about it.

I think with the change in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/36435 "LU-12811 ptlrpc: pass buffer size to the swabbing functions" we could add the RMF_F_NO_SIZE_CHECK flag for quotactl_only and have it verify the buffer size is large enough for the base struct obd_quotactl, and optionally has qc_poolname.

--- Message (Hongchao Zhang, 2020-04-25 14:25:42.000000000) ---
Patch Set 50:

Is the pool quota only enforced when the global quota is set?
It's better to print some information for "lfs quota" to inform the user about it,
or adding some check in "qmt_set" to set some quota limits according to the pool quota?

--- Message (Andreas Dilger, 2020-04-25 18:38:53.000000000) ---
Patch Set 50: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

One bug that needs to be fixed. 

For sanity-quota.sh I had only intended that the new tests be changed to use lower-case variables for locals, but if you've changed all of the tests that is OK too (normally such a large and unrelated change should be done in a separate cleanup patch).

--- Message (Sergey Cheremencev, 2020-04-27 19:07:46.000000000) ---
Patch Set 50:

> Is the pool quota only enforced when the global quota is set?
 > It's better to print some information for "lfs quota" to inform the
 > user about it,
 > or adding some check in "qmt_set" to set some quota limits
 > according to the pool quota?

Good note. I thought about that but didn't find nice solution.
At the 1st case we for example can return EINVAL in case if user sets limits to quota pools despite there is no any hard/soft limits for "global" pool. But there is a chance that hard/soft limit for global pool becomes zero later and user will not understand why limits for quota pools stopped working.
Another case - if global pool lqe hasn't limits we can set some huge value(2**32) in had limit, together with quota pool limit. I.e. each time when we set any limits for quota pool, we need to check global pool limit and set it if it is zero. But I am afraid users may be scared with this huge limit in "lfs quota" output that they didn't set.
Honestly, I am voting just to print warning message when user sets limit for quota pools, smth like: "Please, check that user has any "global" limit, otherwise quota pools may not work properly".
Andreas, Shaun, James, what do you think?

--- Message (James Simmons, 2020-04-27 20:23:41.000000000) ---

--- Message (Andreas Dilger, 2020-07-31 02:41:05.000000000) ---
Patch Set 52:

(6 comments)

I noticed when reviewing the operations manual change that the use of "lfs setquota -o <pool>" for the short option is confusing, because "lfs quota -o <ost>" is a totally different thing.

Since this patch is already landed, could you please submit a small patch to remove the use of "-o" as the short option for quota pools, and instead have no short option at all.

This also needs to be updated in the test scripts (if any use of "-o" is there) and in the lfs-setquota.1 man page.

Using --pool is not so long to type each time, and "lfs find --pool" also does not have a short option.
